passage: If the builders of the Legendary Tower of Babel _____(1) a work crew from Utah, the massive structure might actually have been completed _____(2) collapsing in the confusion of the workers' diverse languages. The linguistically savvy Utahans could have worked _____(3) bees in a hive. Or at least that is the boast among modern-day locals, who are using their language skills to build the economy of their home state. _____(4) populated, landlocked and laced with deserts, mountains and rugged wilderness regions typical of the American west, Utah is an unlikely place to find people _____(5) collectively speak 90 percent of the world's written languages.
question_text: If the builders..._____(1) a work crew from Utah
